name: admin-bar-item
summary: Add an item to the WordPress admin bar via a Ready event listener.
description: Scaffolds a Ready listener that hooks admin_bar_menu and adds a node with a configurable title, link, and tooltip.
problem: You want a quick-access shortcut in the wp-admin top bar (status indicator, deep link, action button).
appliesWhen: Logged-in users need quick access to a plugin feature
The shortcut should be visible across both the front-end bar and wp-admin
avoidWhen: The shortcut is logged-out only (admin bar isn't shown to anonymous users)
You need a complex UI element (admin bar nodes are simple links)
examples: Add a 'Sync Now' shortcut to the admin bar
Surface a status indicator on the admin bar
tradeoffs: One class plus a listener registration. Admin bar nodes are simple — for richer interaction, use an admin page and link to it from here.
outputs: A {name} class implementing CanHandle that hooks admin_bar_menu
A getListeners() entry mapping Ready to {name}
postApply: Edit shouldRender() to gate visibility correctly
Edit href() to point at the right URL
Add child nodes if you need a dropdown (call $bar->add_node multiple times with parent = nodeId)

nodeId.description: Unique admin bar node id (lowercase, dash-separated)
